content.text:
I'm Claude Code, ready to assist with your codebase. I've reviewed the project context and understand:

**Project Overview:**
- This is an experimental proving ground for Claude Code configuration development
- Primary focus: **Sidekick hook system** (modular, pluggable, dual-scope compatible)
- Status: Production-ready with plugin architecture and dependency resolution
- Current branch: `feat/sidekick-phase1`

**Key Constraints I'll Follow:**
- Law 0–3 hierarchy (codebase integrity > functional safety > user instructions > maintainability)
- Semantic commits with pre-commit checks
- Never destructive git operations without explicit written approval
- No file modifications outside project directory
- Test before committing; format before pushing
- Succinctness (don't waste tokens)
- Verify your assertions—trust but verify

**I'm Ready For:**
- File searches and codebase navigation
- Code review and analysis
- Architecture and design discussions
- Test execution and debugging
- Installation/deployment guidance (with your explicit approval)

What would you like me to tackle?

message.content: we're going to test the different LLM providers one at a time in the same session.  Right now, I believe we're configured to use claude CLI, so let's see if that is working

content.thinking:
The user wants to test the different LLM providers one at a time in the same session. They believe they're currently configured to use Claude CLI and want to verify that it's working.

Looking at the context, the Sidekick system has pluggable LLM providers that support:
- claude-cli (default)
- openai-api
- gemini-cli
- custom

The LLM providers are used in the topic-extraction feature for conversation analysis and resume generation.

To test if Claude CLI is currently working, I should:
1. First check the current configuration to confirm what LLM provider is configured
2. Then verify that the Claude CLI is actually working by potentially running a simple test

Let me check the configuration first. I should look at:
- The project's sidekick.conf (if it exists)
- The user's sidekick.conf
- The defaults in config.defaults

Then we can test it by either:
- Looking at recent logs from sidekick operations
- Running a manual test of the LLM provider
- Triggering a topic extraction by submitting a prompt

Let me start by checking the configuration.

content.text: I'll help you verify the Claude CLI LLM provider is working correctly. Let me check the current configuration first.

content.content:
1→# ============================================================================
     2→# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
     3→# ============================================================================
     4→#
     5→# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
     6→# Values can be overridden in:
     7→#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
     8→#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
     9→#
    10→# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
    11→#
    12→# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)
    13→
    14→# ============================================================================
    15→# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
    16→# ============================================================================
    17→
    18→# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
    19→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
    20→
    21→# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
    22→FEATURE_RESUME=true
    23→
    24→# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
    25→FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true
    26→
    27→# Tracking: Request counting
    28→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
    29→
    30→# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
    31→FEATURE_REMINDER=true
    32→
    33→# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
    34→FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
    35→
    36→# ============================================================================
    37→# LLM PROVIDER CONFIGURATION
    38→# ============================================================================
    39→
    40→# LLM provider selection
    41→# Options: claude-cli | openai-api | gemini-cli | custom
    42→LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli
    43→
    44→# ============================================================================
    45→# CLAUDE CLI PROVIDER
    46→#
    47→# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
    48→# -haiku (4.5)  $1.00 / $5.00
    49→# -sonnet (4.5) $3.00 / $15.00  (for in/out < 200k tokens)
    50→# -opus (4.1)   $15.00 / $75
    51→# ============================================================================
    52→
    53→# Path to Claude CLI binary (auto-detect if empty)
    54→# Searches: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
    55→LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=
    56→
    57→# Model name for Claude provider (used when LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli)
    58→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
    59→LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku
    60→
    61→# ============================================================================
    62→# OPENAI API PROVIDER
    63→#
    64→# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
    65→#
    66→# -gpt-5-nano  $0.05 / $0.40
    67→# -gpt-o4-mini $0.15 / $0.60
    68→# -gpt-5-mini  $0.25 / $2.00
    69→# ============================================================================
    70→
    71→# OpenAI API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api)
    72→# Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
    73→LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=
    74→
    75→# OpenAI API endpoint
    76→LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions
    77→
    78→# Model name for OpenAI provider
    79→# Options: gpt-o4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano
    80→LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-nano
    81→
    82→# ============================================================================
    83→# GEMINI CLI PROVIDER
    84→#
    85→# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
    86→# -gemini-2.5-flash-lite $0.10 / 0.40
    87→# -gemini-2.5-flash      $0.30 / 2.50
    88→# -gemini-2.5-pro        $1.25 / $10.00 (in/out tokens < 200k)
    89→# ============================================================================
    90→
    91→# Path to Gemini CLI binary
    92→LLM_GEMINI_BIN=gemini
    93→
    94→# Model name for Gemini provider
    95→# Options: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite, gemini-2.5-pro
    96→LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite
    97→
    98→# ============================================================================
    99→# CUSTOM PROVIDER
   100→# ============================================================================
   101→
   102→# Custom command template with placeholders:
   103→#   {BIN} - binary path (from LLM_CUSTOM_BIN)
   104→#   {MODEL} - model name (from LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL)
   105→#   {PROMPT_FILE} - path to temporary file containing prompt
   106→#   {TIMEOUT} - timeout in seconds
   107→# Example: {BIN} --model {MODEL} --timeout {TIMEOUT} < {PROMPT_FILE}
   108→LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND=
   109→
   110→# Binary path for custom provider
   111→LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=
   112→
   113→# Model name for custom provider
   114→LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=default
   115→
   116→# ============================================================================
   117→# TOPIC EXTRACTION
   118→# ============================================================================
   119→
   120→# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
   121→# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
   122→# Model to use for topic extraction
   123→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
   124→TOPIC_MODEL=haiku
   125→
   126→# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
   127→TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80
   128→
   129→# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
   130→TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true
   131→
   132→# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
   133→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
   134→
   135→# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
   136→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1
   137→
   138→# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
   139→# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
   140→# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
   141→TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
   142→
   143→# ============================================================================
   144→# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
   145→# ============================================================================
   146→
   147→# Enable sleeper process
   148→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   149→
   150→# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
   151→# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
   152→SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600
   153→
   154→# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
   155→SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
   156→
   157→# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
   158→SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10
   159→
   160→# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
   161→# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
   162→# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
   163→# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
   164→# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
   165→SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
   166→SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20
   167→
   168→# ============================================================================
   169→# RESUME (Session Continuity)
   170→# ============================================================================
   171→
   172→# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
   173→# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
   174→# Model to use for resume snarkification
   175→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
   176→RESUME_MODEL=haiku
   177→
   178→# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
   179→# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
   180→RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5
   181→
   182→# ============================================================================
   183→# STATUSLINE
   184→# ============================================================================
   185→
   186→# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
   187→# Statusline will show percentage of this value
   188→STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000
   189→
   190→# ============================================================================
   191→# TRACKING
   192→# ============================================================================
   193→
   194→# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)
   195→
   196→# ============================================================================
   197→# REMINDER
   198→# ============================================================================
   199→
   200→# Static reminder cadence (responses)
   201→# Reminder will be injected every N responses
   202→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   203→
   204→# ============================================================================
   205→# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
   206→# ============================================================================
   207→
   208→# Enable automatic cleanup
   209→# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
   210→CLEANUP_ENABLED=true
   211→
   212→# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
   213→# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
   214→CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5
   215→
   216→# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
   217→# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
   218→CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2
   219→
   220→# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
   221→# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
   222→CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false
   223→
   224→# ============================================================================
   225→# GLOBAL
   226→# ============================================================================
   227→
   228→# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
   229→# Controls verbosity of log output
   230→#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
   231→#   info: Informational and above
   232→#   warn: Warnings and errors only
   233→#   error: Errors only
   234→LOG_LEVEL=info
   235→
   236→# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
   237→# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
   238→CLAUDE_BIN=
   239→

file.content:
# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
FEATURE_REMINDER=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# LLM PROVIDER CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# LLM provider selection
# Options: claude-cli | openai-api | gemini-cli | custom
LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -haiku (4.5)  $1.00 / $5.00
# -sonnet (4.5) $3.00 / $15.00  (for in/out < 200k tokens)
# -opus (4.1)   $15.00 / $75
# ============================================================================

# Path to Claude CLI binary (auto-detect if empty)
# Searches: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=

# Model name for Claude provider (used when LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli)
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku

# ============================================================================
# OPENAI API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
#
# -gpt-5-nano  $0.05 / $0.40
# -gpt-o4-mini $0.15 / $0.60
# -gpt-5-mini  $0.25 / $2.00
# ============================================================================

# OpenAI API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api)
# Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=

# OpenAI API endpoint
LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions

# Model name for OpenAI provider
# Options: gpt-o4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano
LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-nano

# ============================================================================
# GEMINI CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -gemini-2.5-flash-lite $0.10 / 0.40
# -gemini-2.5-flash      $0.30 / 2.50
# -gemini-2.5-pro        $1.25 / $10.00 (in/out tokens < 200k)
# ============================================================================

# Path to Gemini CLI binary
LLM_GEMINI_BIN=gemini

# Model name for Gemini provider
# Options: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite, gemini-2.5-pro
LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite

# ============================================================================
# CUSTOM PROVIDER
# ============================================================================

# Custom command template with placeholders:
#   {BIN} - binary path (from LLM_CUSTOM_BIN)
#   {MODEL} - model name (from LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL)
#   {PROMPT_FILE} - path to temporary file containing prompt
#   {TIMEOUT} - timeout in seconds
# Example: {BIN} --model {MODEL} --timeout {TIMEOUT} < {PROMPT_FILE}
LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND=

# Binary path for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=

# Model name for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=default

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80

# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)

# ============================================================================
# REMINDER
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

   160→# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
   161→# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
   162→# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
   163→# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
   164→# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
   165→SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
   166→SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20
   167→
   168→# ============================================================================
   169→# RESUME (Session Continuity)
   170→# ============================================================================
   171→
   172→# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
   173→# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
   174→# Model to use for resume snarkification
   175→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
   176→RESUME_MODEL=haiku
   177→
   178→# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
   179→# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
   180→RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5
   181→
   182→# ============================================================================
   183→# STATUSLINE
   184→# ============================================================================
   185→
   186→# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
   187→# Statusline will show percentage of this value
   188→STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000
   189→
   190→# ============================================================================
   191→# TRACKING
   192→# ============================================================================
   193→
   194→# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)
   195→
   196→# ============================================================================
   197→# REMINDER
   198→# ============================================================================
   199→
   200→# Static reminder cadence (responses)
   201→# Reminder will be injected every N responses
   202→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   203→
   204→# ============================================================================
   205→# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
   206→# ============================================================================
   207→
   208→# Enable automatic cleanup
   209→# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
   210→CLEANUP_ENABLED=true
   211→
   212→# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
   213→# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
   214→CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5
   215→
   216→# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
   217→# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
   218→CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2
   219→
   220→# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
   221→# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
   222→CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false
   223→
   224→# ============================================================================
   225→# GLOBAL
   226→# ============================================================================

   232→#   warn: Warnings and errors only

file.content:
# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
FEATURE_REMINDER=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# LLM PROVIDER CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# LLM provider selection
# Options: claude-cli | openai-api | gemini-cli | custom
LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -haiku (4.5)  $1.00 / $5.00
# -sonnet (4.5) $3.00 / $15.00  (for in/out < 200k tokens)
# -opus (4.1)   $15.00 / $75
# ============================================================================

# Path to Claude CLI binary (auto-detect if empty)
# Searches: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=

# Model name for Claude provider (used when LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli)
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku

# ============================================================================
# OPENAI API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
#
# -gpt-5-nano  $0.05 / $0.40
# -gpt-o4-mini $0.15 / $0.60
# -gpt-5-mini  $0.25 / $2.00
# ============================================================================

# OpenAI API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api)
# Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=

# OpenAI API endpoint
LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions

# Model name for OpenAI provider
# Options: gpt-o4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano
LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-nano

# ============================================================================
# GEMINI CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -gemini-2.5-flash-lite $0.10 / 0.40
# -gemini-2.5-flash      $0.30 / 2.50
# -gemini-2.5-pro        $1.25 / $10.00 (in/out tokens < 200k)
# ============================================================================

# Path to Gemini CLI binary
LLM_GEMINI_BIN=gemini

# Model name for Gemini provider
# Options: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite, gemini-2.5-pro
LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite

# ============================================================================
# CUSTOM PROVIDER
# ============================================================================

# Custom command template with placeholders:
#   {BIN} - binary path (from LLM_CUSTOM_BIN)
#   {MODEL} - model name (from LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL)
#   {PROMPT_FILE} - path to temporary file containing prompt
#   {TIMEOUT} - timeout in seconds
# Example: {BIN} --model {MODEL} --timeout {TIMEOUT} < {PROMPT_FILE}
LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND=

# Binary path for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=

# Model name for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=default

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80

# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)

# ============================================================================
# REMINDER
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=
